sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Merck & Co. Inc. stock (US58933Y1055): Is its oncology dominance strong enough to unlock new upside?

The article also touches on analyst views, risks, and key factors investors should monitor.

Its wide economic moat, built on high switching costs, scale economies, and extensive R&D, ensures sustained profitability and resilience against market volatility for U.S. and English-speaking investors.

feed.title: What To Expect From U.S. Bancorp’s (USB) Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (USB) is set to announce its Q1 earnings this Thursday morning. Despite some past revenue misses, investor sentiment in the diversified banks segment is positive, and U.S. Bancorp's stock has risen 9.1% over the last month.

feed.title: AI-powered mainframe exits are a bubble set to pop: Gartner
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that over 70% of AI-powered mainframe exit projects initiated in 2026 will fail due to overestimating generative AI capabilities, and 75% of vendors in this market will either pivot or cease to exist by 2030. The firm highlights that mainframe migrations are complex due to mission-critical applications and vast interconnected data, issues that generative AI struggles with beyond debt detection. Gartner suggests that aggressive investor demand is pushing vendors to deploy AI where it's not optimal, cautioning against "magical solution" promises and promoting a platform-smart approach instead.

feed.title: Dexcom Inc. stock (US2521311074): Why does its continuous glucose monitoring edge matter more now?
feed.summary: Dexcom Inc. is a leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) systems, with its technology addressing the needs of over 38 million Americans with diabetes. The company's business model relies on recurring revenue from sensor replacements and is expanding its market to include Type 2 diabetics and non-diabetics interested in metabolic health. Despite competition, Dexcom maintains a strong competitive edge due to superior accuracy, strategic partnerships, and a focus on innovation and market expansion in the US and English-speaking countries.

feed.title: Boeing Company stock (US0970231058): Does commercial aviation recovery now drive the real turnaround?

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. stock (US58933Y1055): Is Keytruda's dominance strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. relies heavily on its blockbuster cancer drug Keytruda, which accounts for over 40% of its sales, raising questions about sustained growth amidst patent cliffs and competition. While Keytruda's label expansions and other products like Gardasil and Winrevair provide diversification, the looming patent expiry in 2028 poses a significant risk. Analysts generally view Merck positively due to its strong fundamentals and pipeline, but recommend monitoring trial readouts, regulatory decisions, and market reactions to maintain its competitive edge.

feed.title: Reese’s candy heir rips Hershey for changing recipes
feed.summary: The grandson of Reese's Peanut Butter Cups creator, Brad Reese, has again criticized Hershey for changing its candy recipes, specifically alleging the quiet substitution of milk chocolate with compound coating made with vegetable oils. Hershey plans to return to using milk and dark chocolate by 2027, but Reese dismisses this as a "PR stunt," stating they could revert in three days. He expressed strong disappointment, even spitting out a Reese’s Mini Hearts Unwrapped due to its quality.

feed.title: Broadridge’s Unified Digital Asset Platform Could Be A Game Changer For Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR)

This initiative aims to strengthen Broadridge's role in digital governance and back-office processing, extending its existing tokenization infrastructure into wealth management. feed.title: Altria Group Inc. stock (US02209S1033): Is its smoke-free pivot strong enough to unlock new upside?
feed.summary: Altria Group Inc. (US02209S1033) is attempting to pivot from traditional cigarettes to smoke-free products like `on!` nicotine pouches and e-vapor, aiming for $3 billion in annual revenue from these alternatives by 2025. While Altria offers a high-yield dividend for income-focused investors, its long-term growth hinges on the success of this smoke-free transition amidst declining cigarette volumes and regulatory challenges. Investors should monitor the company's ability to scale new products and navigate FDA actions, balancing its reliable dividend income with the uncertainty of its transformative strategy.

feed.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Williams-Sonoma (WSM) After Its Strong Multi-Year Rally?
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) has seen a strong multi-year rally, prompting a valuation analysis. While a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggests the stock is undervalued by 20.2% at US$242.80 compared to its current price of US$193.84, its P/E ratio of 21.2x is above both the industry and peer averages, indicating it might be overvalued through that lens. The article also presents "Bull" and "Bear" case narratives for Williams-Sonoma, with fair values ranging from US$230.00 to US$153.30, providing different perspectives for investors to consider.

feed.title: BRP Suspends FY27 Guidance Due to Changes to U.S. Tariff Environment
feed.summary: BRP Inc. announced the suspension of its full-year FY27 guidance following changes to U.S. Section 232 tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper imports, effective April 6, 2026. These amendments impose a 25% tariff on the total value of imported snowmobiles and most ORV models, replacing earlier tariffs on metal content only. The company anticipates potential incremental tariff costs exceeding $500 million for the remainder of the year but expects to manage the impact through its strong balance sheet and operational agility.
